QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_20

question.text: A One Person Company (OPC) incorporated under the Companies Act 2013 applies for merchant banker registration. What will SEBI's response be?

answer.text: SEBI will grant registration as a Category II MB
SEBI will grant registration if the OPC meets net worth requirements
SEBI will grant conditional registration for 1 year
SEBI will reject the application since OPCs are explicitly excluded from eligibility

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI regulations, a One Person Company (OPC) is explicitly excluded from the definition of eligible entities that can register as a merchant banker. The exclusions are: (I) body corporate incorporated outside India (unless RBI-licensed foreign bank), (II) One Person Company, and (III) NBFC. **The Trap:** Since an OPC is a valid company under the Companies Act 2013, candidates might assume it qualifies as a "body corporate" — but the regulation specifically carves out OPCs as an exception. **Cross-Exam Context:** Entity type restrictions for SEBI registration are tested across NISM Series IX, X-A, and X-B with varying exclusion lists. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you are structuring a new entity for MB registration, opt for a private limited company or LLP — never an OPC.
